expect: Cell blueprint reduces boot time >=20% by skipping orient computation. Baseline: orient.py takes 15-30s, dispatch_optimizer 10-20s, total ~60s compute.
actual: Boot-time bottleneck is NOT orient computation but concurrent artifact absorption. 76% of sessions (23/30) start with absorb/commit as first commit. Session span median=1163s (19min), mean=1409s (23min). Boot gap (inter-session, when positive): median=233s, mean=346s (n=8). Cell blueprint pre-computes orient but misses the primary bottleneck: identifying what concurrent sessions produced since last commit.
diff: Expected orient-computation as bottleneck: FALSIFIED. Actual bottleneck is concurrent artifact absorption (76% first-commit rate). Cell blueprint needs enhancement: add untracked_artifacts field. L-1184 prediction should be REFRAMED: boot-time reduction through artifact-pre-identification, not orient-skipping.

tool_validation.merge_compatibility.py: self-test passes, 5 dimensions computed, zone classification correct

enhancement_proposal: cell_blueprint.py save should include git status --short as untracked_artifacts field
